title: North Dakota: no general deletion right yet
summary: As of mid-2026, North Dakota has not enacted a comprehensive consumer-privacy law granting a general right to delete personal data held by data brokers. For removal, rely on the data broker's own opt-out (below), which works regardless of where you live.
disclaimer: General information, not legal advice, and not yet independently reviewed. State privacy laws change quickly — verify whether your state has enacted new rights before relying on this.
